title: Rural 340B Access Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill allows rural emergency hospitals to buy medicines at lower prices through a special government program called 340B. Rural emergency hospitals are small hospitals in country areas that mostly help people in emergencies and let them stay for less than 24 hours. To join this program, these hospitals must be owned by the government, be nonprofit organizations with government powers, or be private nonprofits that agree to help poor patients who don't have Medicare or Medicaid. The 340B program makes drug companies sell medicines at cheaper prices to hospitals that help low-income people.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Rural 340B Access Act of 2025.

sections.title: Section 2  Including Rural Emergency Hospitals as Covered Entities for Purposes of the 340B Drug Discount Program
sections.text: This section changes an existing law about a program called 340B that helps hospitals buy medicines at lower prices. The change adds rural emergency hospitals to the list of hospitals that can get these discounted medicines. To qualify, a rural emergency hospital must be owned or run by state or local government. It could also be a nonprofit organization that has been given government powers by the state or local government. Another way to qualify is if it is a private nonprofit rural emergency hospital that has a contract with state or local government to provide health care to low income people who do not have Medicare or Medicaid.
